name: PHP
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in PHP as a competency should be able to write, read and debug modern PHP 8.x code with strict/weak typing, scalar-union-nullable types, typed properties, attributes, closures, generators, SPL interfaces, understand value vs reference semantics, GC, CLI vs FPM, ini/env config, namespaces, PSR-4 autoloading, composer classmap optimisation, traits, late-static-binding, magic methods, reflection and structured Throwable/Error/Exception hierarchies. They apply SOLID, composition over inheritance and GoF patterns (factory, strategy, adapter, decorator, observer, repository), wire dependencies with containers, manage service lifetimes, marshal middleware or event-bus pipelines and refactor legacy procedural code into modular OO. They leverage SPL data structures, streams, sockets, cURL, intl, mbstring, DateTime, JSON, XML, zip, openssl, build robust CLI tools with getopt or symfony/console and parse or emit JSON, XML, CSV, YAML under schema validation. They develop full-stack apps in mainstream frameworks such as Laravel, Symfony, Slim or Mezzio handling routing, controllers, request/response abstractions, service providers, middleware chains, templating, migrations, seeders, factories, ORM relationships, query builders, transactions, events, queues, schedulers, notifications, mailers, storage abstractions and author custom extensions (commands, macros, validation rules, directives, packages). They design relational schemas to 3NF with keys, indexes, constraints, cascades, JSON columns, tune queries via EXPLAIN and covering indexes, mitigate N+1, use prepared statements, hydration or raw SQL through repositories, choose key-value or document stores when fit and build bulk import/export pipelines with transactional integrity. They command the HTTP spec—methods, status codes, headers, caching directives, content negotiation, CORS, range, multipart, chunked—craft RESTful and GraphQL APIs with versioning, pagination, HATEOAS, employ websockets, SSE or long-polling and manage cookies, sessions and token lifecycles. They enforce OWASP Top-10 defences, implement Argon2id/bcrypt hashing, 2FA, CSRF tokens, CSP, HSTS, SameSite cookies, JWT/OAuth2/OIDC flows, secure composer supply chain and guard against deserialisation, SSRF, file upload, path traversal and command injection. They practise TDD/BDD with PHPUnit, Pest or phpspec, employ mocks and E2E suites, monitor coverage, mutation tests, static analysis (PHPStan, Psalm), style tools (PHPCS, PHP-CS-Fixer), automated hooks and CI, debug with Xdebug, profile via Blackfire/Tideways, emit structured JSON logs and instrument APM or OpenTelemetry. They optimise performance with opcache, preloading, authoritative classmaps, Redis/Memcached caches, stampede protection, parallelism via forks, queues, Swoole/ReactPHP, HTTP compression and asset pipelines while eliminating query and payload bottlenecks. They containerise with Docker/Kubernetes, craft multi-stage builds, health/readiness probes, secrets, build CI/CD pipelines with zero-downtime migrations, blue-green releases, rollbacks and backups, tune web-server/PHP-FPM, automate SSL, composer--no-dev deployments and observe via dashboards, log aggregation and alerts. They collaborate through disciplined code reviews, PSR-1/4/7/12/15/17 compliance, PHPDoc, ADRs, OpenAPI specs, estimate tasks, mentor juniors, plan refactors, manage semantic versioning, feature flags and upgrade codebases (PHP 7→8). They integrate external payment, messaging, storage, analytics, search and AI services via SDK or raw HTTP using retries, circuit breakers and idempotency keys, support i18n/l10n with ICU pluralisation and timezone handling, maintain accessibility and SEO in templates and operate confidently in both monoliths and micro-services.
long_scope:
INTERMEDIATE PHP – Competency Scope (3 – 5 years professional experience)

An engineer operating at this level is expected to work with limited supervision on medium-sized modules, contribute to overall system quality, and reliably extend or refactor production code written by others.  Depth in fundamentals is already secure; breadth now expands to the full professional tool-chain, modern language features up to the current LTS version, and day-to-day architectural considerations of a typical web application stack.

The scope below organises what such a developer must demonstrably command.  Every statement is an integral part of the competency; none are merely illustrative or optional.  Questions written from this scope should therefore probe for applied understanding, not rote definition.

────────────────────────────────────────
1. Language Core & Runtime Behaviour
• Complete knowledge of scalar, compound and special types; strict vs. weak typing; scalar, union and nullable type declarations; typed class properties; attributes; value vs. reference semantics; garbage collection; memory-usage patterns.  
• Mastery of functions, variadics, anonymous functions / closures, generators, yield delegation, and typical SPL interfaces behind them (Iterator, ArrayAccess, Countable, etc.).  
• Correct application of control structures, operators (coalescing, spaceship, null-safe), string interpolation rules, execution modes (CLI, FPM), configuration via ini and env settings.  
• Facility with namespaces, autoloading via PSR-4, and composer classmap optimisation; ability to debug autoload failures.  
• Construction of reusable traits, awareness of diamond-inheritance hazards, resolution order; understanding of static context, late-static-binding, magic methods and reflection.  
• Structured exception hierarchy using Throwable/Error/Exception; crafting domain-specific exceptions; best-practice try/catch/finally flow; SPL exceptions and error handler interplay.

2. Object-Oriented & Modular Design
• Application of SOLID principles and common GoF patterns (factory, strategy, adapter, decorator, observer, repository) to typical business logic.  
• Composition over inheritance; dependency injection via container or constructor; awareness of service-lifetime scopes (singleton, request, transient).  
• Encapsulation of cross-cutting concerns via middleware / pipeline or event/bus mechanisms; separation of domain layer from infrastructure and presentation.  
• Ability to evolve legacy procedural code toward modular OO without functional regressions.

3. Standard Library, Extensions & Native Interfaces
• Confident use of SPL data structures, iterators, file handling, stream wrappers, sockets, cURL, intl, mbstring, DateTime/DateInterval/DatePeriod, JSON, XML, zip, hash, filter, openssl.  
• Design and consumption of CLI commands with getopt, symfony/console (or equivalent), coloured output, exit codes and signal handling.  
• Efficient parsing & generation of JSON, XML, CSV, YAML with schema or document validation.

4. Framework & Ecosystem Fluency
• Proficient building blocks in a mainstream MVC / HMVC / micro-kernel PHP framework: routing, controllers, request/response abstraction, dependency container, service providers, middleware stack, configuration layers, environmental separation, templating engine.  
• Creation of database migrations, seeders, factories; management of model relationships, query-builder pipelines, eager/lazy loading, scopes; transactional boundaries and rollback.  
• Event dispatching, listener registration, queues, job retries, scheduling, notifications, mailers, storage abstractions.  
• Extension points: custom console commands, macros, validation rules, template directives, middleware, service tags, packages.  
• Capability to read, debug and contribute to community packages; respect for semantic versioning.

5. Data Persistence & Integration
• Relational schema design to 3NF; composite keys; indices, constraints, cascades, enum emulation; JSON/Array columns; view and materialised view creation.  
• Query tuning using EXPLAIN plans, slow-query logs, covering indices, pagination strategies; mitigation of N + 1; batching and chunking of large data sets.  
• Use of parameterised queries, prepared statements, ORM hydration vs. raw SQL mapping, repository pattern decoupling, NoSQL key-value and document stores for specific use-cases.  
• Data import/export pipelines, CSV streaming, bulk loaders, transactional integrity with external systems.

6. HTTP, Web Protocols & API Craft
• Deep understanding of the HTTP spec: methods, status codes (100-5xx), headers, caching directives, content negotiation, range requests, multipart forms, chunked encoding, CORS preflight.  
• RESTful resource modelling, HATEOAS optionality, API versioning, pagination, filtering, partial responses; GraphQL schema and resolver basics.  
• Web-socket handshake, event broadcasting, server-sent events; long-poll vs. push channels.  
• Cookie, session and token lifecycles in stateless vs. stateful API scenarios.

7. Security Responsibilities
• Practical application of the OWASP Top-10 for PHP: input validation, output escaping, CSRF tokens, XSS contexts (HTML, JS, CSS, attribute), SQL/NoSQL injection, command injection, SSRF, file upload validation, path traversal, deserialisation attacks.  
• Password hashing policies (Argon2id, bcrypt), credential rotation, account-locking, 2FA integration, secure remember-me.  
• Transport security: HTTPS enforcement, HSTS, secure cookies, SameSite attributes, Content-Security-Policy, Referrer-Policy, feature-policy.  
• JWT/OAuth2/OpenID Connect token inspection, refresh flows, scope enforcement.  
• Secure dependency management: composer audit, signed tags, supply-chain awareness.

8. Quality, Testing & Observability
• Test-driven or behaviour-driven workflows with PHPUnit, Pest or phpspec; red-green-refactor discipline; isolation via mocks/stubs/fakes; integration and end-to-end suites.  
• Code coverage analysis, mutation testing, static analysis (PHPStan/Psalm), code style (PHP-CS-Fixer, PHPCS), automated pre-commit hooks and CI pipelines.  
• Runtime debugging with Xdebug, step-through, profiling, flame-graphs, memory leaks; logging strategy (structured JSON, correlation IDs), error reporting, alerting thresholds.  
• APM and tracing—configuring and interpreting output from tools such as Blackfire, Tideways, NewRelic, OpenTelemetry.

9. Performance & Scalability
• Opcode caching, preloading, autoloader optimisation, classmap authorities.  
• Application-level caching with in-memory stores, tag-based invalidation, cache warming, stampede protection, TTL strategy, cache-aside vs. write-through.  
• Parallelism via process forking, task queues, asynchronous I/O libraries (Swoole, ReactPHP) where appropriate; comprehension of thread-safety constraints.  
• Profiling to locate bottlenecks, query optimisation, payload reduction, HTTP compression, asset pipeline integration.

10. Dev-Ops, Deployment & Lifecycle
• Environment replication with Docker Compose or Kubernetes manifests, multi-stage builds, health-checks, readiness & liveness probes, volume and secret management.  
• CI/CD pipeline configuration, build artefact versioning, zero-downtime migrations, blue-green or canary releases, rollbacks, database backups and restores.  
• Web server/PHP-FPM configuration tuning (worker counts, max-children, request timeouts), log rotation, process supervision, SSL certificate automation.  
• Composer deployment practices (–no-dev, classmap-authoritative); configuration of environment variables, secret management stores.  
• Monitoring dashboards, metric thresholds, log aggregation, incident post-mortem culture.

11. Maintenance & Collaboration
• Systematic code-review processes, constructive feedback, adherence to PSR-1/PSR-12/PSR-4/PSR-7/PSR-15/PSR-17.  
• Documentation discipline: PHPDoc, README/ADR/architecture decision records, OpenAPI specs, onboarding guides.  
• Estimation and task breakdown, sprint participation, mentoring juniors, raising technical debt tickets, refactoring plans.  
• Handling of version upgrades (7→8.x), deprecation policies, feature flags, compatibility layers, semantic versioning for internal packages.

12. Professional Breadth
• Integration with external payment, messaging, storage, analytics, search, and AI services through SDK or raw HTTP; resilience via retries, circuit-breakers, idempotency keys.  
• Multi-locale & timezone support: ICU message formatting, pluralisation rules, currency formatting, RTL layouts.  
• Accessibility and SEO considerations in server-rendered templates and API responses.  
• Exposure to micro-service boundaries, shared libraries, event-driven choreography, yet still able to produce value in a monolith.

────────────────────────────────────────

An interviewer evaluating an INTERMEDIATE PHP developer should therefore probe across all of the above domains, expecting confident explanations, practical anecdotes, and code-level reasoning.  Depth comparable to an advanced specialist is not required in highly niche topics (e.g., internal engine hacking, JIT tuning, exotic extensions), but comfort with the entire modern PHP ecosystem and daily professional responsibilities is mandatory.
